claim: Crystallized knowledge whose enforcing assumption has died shall be surfaced as stale (enforced-but-wrong, a bad habit).
why: SETTLED (P6): the structural detection path now exists. The stale-substrate signal is produced by ReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer (internal/diagnose/finding.go): for each SETTLED+ENFORCED Requirement whose Assumptions include a DEAD one, it emits a Finding (Condition="reflect_dead_assumption_on_enforcer", Target=requirement id) whose Imperative names the dead assumption and the requirement resting on it -- surfacing the bad-habit case where an enforced rule's premise has quietly died. This Finding flows through AllFindings into the what-now attention signals (internal/diagnose/signal.go) via the same registry as every other reflection predicate (R-reflection-predicates-first-class). Today the hotam-spec-self domain carries zero DEAD assumptions, so no stale substrate actually fires; the structural detection path is in place for when one does. TestReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer_Fires (internal/diagnose/finding_test.go) pins the firing path; TestReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer_ProseNotEnforced pins that PROSE requirements do not trigger it.
